chunk_summary: This section discusses pharmacotherapy for obesity management, focusing on dosing and safety monitoring of orlistat, metformin, and GLP-1 receptor agonists, including semaglutide and liraglutide, alongside lifestyle interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the dosing protocols for orlistat, metformin, and GLP-1 receptor agonists in obesity management.
Identify safety monitoring requirements and adverse effects for each medication.
Evaluate the role of structured lifestyle interventions alongside pharmacotherapy.
Recognize contraindications and precautions for GLP-1 receptor agonists.
key_facts: Orlistat: 120 mg orally three times daily with fat-containing meals; adverse effects include steatorrhea and abdominal cramps.
Semaglutide: Weekly subcutaneous dosing escalates from 0.25 mg to 2.4 mg; STEP TEENS trial showed ~16% BMI reduction over 68 weeks.
Liraglutide: Start at 0.6 mg daily, titrate weekly to 3.0 mg; slower escalation if adverse effects occur.
Metformin: Initial dose 500 mg daily with food, titrate to 1000 mg twice daily; useful for T2DM, PCOS, and antipsychotic-associated weight gain.
GLP-1 receptor agonists: Avoid in patients with medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2; monitor for pancreatitis and gallbladder disease.
reference_quote: In the STEP TEENS trial, semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly plus lifestyle produced a mean BMI reduction of about 16% over 68 weeks. (Reference 3)
document_key_topics: GLP-1 receptor agonist dosing and safety
Role of metformin in obesity-related comorbidities
